articles.title: Bay Street Seen Opening On Mixed Note; BoC, Fed Announcements In Focus
articles.expand: (RTTNews) - Canadian stocks are seen opening on a mixed note Wednesday morning, with investors tracking global cues and the trend in commodity markets, as well as the developments in the Middle East where the war continues to cause concerns.

articles.title: The EURUSD, USDJPY and GBPUSD are little changed to kickstart the Fed rate decision day
articles.expand: The USD is little changed as markets grow increasingly comfortable with developments in the Middle East. At the same time, attention is shifting toward a heavy slate of central bank decisions, highlighted by today’s Fed announcement. As North American traders enter, the dollar is mixed against the major currency pairs, with price action—rather than...

articles.title: Dollar Pressured by Rising Stocks
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) on Tuesday fell by -0.14%. The dollar gave up overnight gains and turned lower on Tuesday as T-note yields fell after the weekly ADP employment change showed the fewest new jobs added in five weeks, a dovish factor for Fed policy. The dollar extended its...

articles.expand: FUNDAMENTAL OVERVIEW USD: The US dollar pulled back across the board in the first part of the week on what looks like profit-taking from extreme levels rather than a change in fundamentals as we haven't got any meaningful catalyst to trigger a reversal yet. articles.title: EUR/USD: Pullback risk on Fed signals – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Francesco Pesole highlights that EUR/USD is driven by war developments and Federal Reserve communication, with a hawkish Dot Plot revision posing downside risks for the pair.

articles.title: USD: Hawkish Fed risk supports Dollar – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale argues that a mildly restrictive Federal Reserve stance and potential hawkish adjustments to the SEP could support the Dollar against G10 and EM currencies.

articles.title: US index futures test key recovery pivots ahead of Fed rate decision
articles.expand: Dow and S P 500 futures lead the recovery into New York, while Nasdaq futures lag slightly, with Fed projections and VIX likely to shape the next move.

articles.title: SNB preview: no changes expected, focus on franc strength
articles.expand: The Swiss National Bank (SNB) is expected to keep interest rates unchanged at 0.0%. The focus will be on central bank's signals about potential intervention in the currency market after EUR/CHF fell to 0.90 recently amid the US-Iran conflict. The central bank has repeatedly said that the bar for a return to negative interest rates is very high even...

articles.title: What to expect from the Fed later today?
articles.expand: It's FOMC day but somehow it does not really feel like that. Or at least not in the typical sense I would say. The Middle East conflict continues to take center stage and even with the central bank bonanza in town this week, markets will stay fixated on US-Iran developments for the most part. Still, it doesn't mean we can ignore what major central ...

articles.title: BoJ meeting preview: Balancing act between growth and inflation as USD/JPY approaches 159.45/161.95 key intervention risk zone
articles.expand: The Bank of Japan is expected to hold rates at 0.75% as it navigates stagflation risks driven by the US–Iran war 2026. Despite weak sentiment reflected in the Nikkei 225, rising wages support a tightening bias. Meanwhile, USD/JPY trades near the 159.45–161.95 intervention zone, keeping markets on edge.

articles.title: BoC poised to keep interest rate unchanged as oil-driven inflation fears linger
articles.expand: The Bank of Canada (BoC) is widely expected to keep its policy rate unchanged at 2.25% at its Wednesday meeting, effectively extending the pause it signalled back in January.

articles.title: Federal Reserve set to stand pat as Iran war complicates interest-rate cuts
articles.expand: The United States (US) Federal Reserve (Fed) announces its interest rate decision on Wednesday, a pivotal meeting for markets to gauge the stance of the world’s most important central bank after an energy shock that could put the Fed’s dual mandate in tension.

articles.title: AUD/USD trades higher slightly above 0.7100, Fed’s policy remains in focus
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair trades marginally higher to near 0.7115 during the European trading session on Wednesday. The Aussie pair is expected to trade broadly sideways as investors await the Federal Reserve’s (Fed) monetary policy outcome at 18:00 GMT.

articles.title: BoJ preview: no changes expected amid lack of inflation progress and geopolitical risk
articles.expand: The Bank of Japan (BoJ) is widely expected to keep interest rates unchanged at 0.75%, as there’s still limited progress on inflation and growing downside risks to the economy linked to the ongoing US-Iran conflict. The latest Tokyo CPI report showed inflation easing further in February, with core inflation slipping below the BoJ’s 2% target. While ...

articles.expand: EUR/USD se mantiene en zona de consolidación antes de la decisión de tipos de la Fed. Se observan niveles de soporte y resistencia técnicos y señales de corrección dentro de la tendencia bajista.

articles.title: From lows to lows: The Indian Rupee’s downward spiral has no end in sight
articles.expand: The Indian Rupee is slipping deeper into uncharted territory, hitting a fresh record low against the US Dollar and keeping the dubious title of Asia's worst-performing currency. With the Iran war raging on and Oil prices increasing, the Rupee’s downward spiral isn’t expected to subside anytime soon.

articles.title: Eurozone CPI finalized at 1.9% in February as price pressures broaden
articles.expand: Eurozone inflation edged higher in February, with headline CPI finalized at 1.9% yoy, up from January’s 1.7% yoy. Core inflation, which strips out energy, food, alcohol and tobacco, also firmed to 2.4% yoy from 2.2% yoy. The composition of inflation highlights a clear shift toward domestically driven pressures. Services were the dominant contributo...

articles.title: BoC: Policy on hold with rangebound Loonie – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale economists expect the BoC to keep its policy rate at 2.25%, despite weaker employment and lower CPI. Markets now price about 33 bps of tightening by year-end after the Iran conflict.

articles.title: EUR/USD struggles to extend recovery above 1.1550, Fed’s policy remains in focus

The major currency pair struggles as the US Dollar strives to regain ground ahead of the Federal Reserve’s (Fed) monetary policy announcement at 18:00 GMT.

articles.title: BoC preview: interest rates to remain unchanged; cautious approach amid US-Iran war
articles.expand: The Bank of Canada (BoC) is expected to keep interest rates steady at 2.25% and maintain a cautious approach amid the US-Iran war and the elevated energy prices.
